title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I'm quitting YouTube and starting my new job. I'm going to use chat GPT.

>> Today, I'm going to be an intern at the global skin care company, Access Y. I'll show you my experience as an office worker here in Seoul, and teach you business English along the way. I'm about to hop on the subway to commute to the office. Luckily, it's a straight shot, so I don't have to transfer to another line.
>> Nice to meet you. So, today you'll be doing some event planning and also finding some influencers. And you're going to help me a lot throughout the day.

But the special part about this cafe is that we also host a lot of offline events here. So we host Christmas parties, community events and stuff like that. Yeah, feel free to come to our cafe whenever you have. I'm going to show you our offline store as well.
How are you intern today? So, my name is Ka. Today, I'm going to help you go through your task of the day. Please let me know if you need anything. I'm going to be always available to help you out.

title: will / shall
examples: >> Today, I'm going to be an intern at the global skin care company, Access Y. I'll show you my experience as an office worker here in Seoul, and teach you business English along the way. I'm about to hop on the subway to commute to the office. Luckily, it's a straight shot, so I don't have to transfer to another line.
>> Nice to meet you. Thank you so much for coming. And I'll be your supervisor today.
>> Nice to meet you. So, today you'll be doing some event planning and also finding some influencers. And you're going to help me a lot throughout the day.

>> I'm planning on going to New York. I'm sure it's going to be really exciting and definitely a good way to practice English. [laughter] >> I'm going to Vietnam. Oh >> yeah, I have a friend living in Hanoi, so I'll go see him for like 5 days. Do you guys have anyone from Vietnam working here?

>> So, a lot of our subscribers are studying English [clears throat] and trying to join a global company. So, do you have any advice? So I think it's very important to know the professional terms in English because especially when you have to talk to people outside of work or even your like managers like it's important to you know keep the boundary and also to know like how to deliver your message in a very professional and polite way. Yeah, >> I do have a very straightforward suggestion. Use AI. The way that you communicate is very important especially in the office setting. So if you're not sure about your writing, you can really use AI. Collaborative, professional, native, all of these adjectives can really help the AI craft your message better. And then you will start learning how to craft professional messages that come across correctly.

examples: >> So, a lot of our subscribers are studying English [clears throat] and trying to join a global company. So, do you have any advice? title: can / could / be able to
examples: >> Okay, >> so here you can find all of our products and we have our brand merchandise here.
A lot of tourists come here actually for shopping and here you can also test all of our products. Here we have a small zone for testing.
>> Can I ask what kind of event is it? Is it like a popup event?
>> We are working on it. So like not the details are confirmed yet but yeah I can just say that we're going to have an event in LA.

>> Hey Kelly. Hi. So, one thing we're going to work on is finding 30 to 35 influencers. You can go through some of the skin care and beauty focused influencers. Since the event is going to be in LA, is really important that you take that into consideration. So, people that are located in LA, you can write down your their name, their Tik Tok or Instagram handle. You can also write down some notes. You want to let us know like some insights about them. You're more than welcome to do so. Okay, >> good luck and see you soon. Okay.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: >> Hi Kelly. You must be Kelly.

title: have to / must
examples: >> Today, I'm going to be an intern at the global skin care company, Access Y. I'll show you my experience as an office worker here in Seoul, and teach you business English along the way. I'm about to hop on the subway to commute to the office. Luckily, it's a straight shot, so I don't have to transfer to another line.
I'm walking to the office now. I was told that Access Y employees have flexible working hours, which is really great because I don't have to clock in at a certain time.

>> Okay, perfect. I know you said you're shipping globally these packages. When is the deadline for that? We have to take into consideration for example the costumes because every country has a different rules. So usually we tend to prepare a list 2 months in advance. We start organizing the shipping list 1 month in advance and with that we coordinate with the logistics team. Um I'm actually planning an event in US and you're going to help him with that as well.
>> Hello. So, today I was granted the points that I can use and like if I'm over and I was over last month, I have to pay my own back. But like I have like a very big discount.

title: should (advice)
examples: >> Okay. Should we do it here or >> uh we can go to the main room and check it together.

title: had better / it's time
examples: >> I got my first task, so it's time to get down to business. [music] I'm going to use chat GPT to do this task.
[music] It's time for my lunch break. I'm having a lunch with the team today. I'm excited and I'm very hungry. Let's go.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: >> Maybe in the beginning you depend on AI more, but then you start to eventually be able to do it yourself. So I would say don't be afraid to spit. No one is going to judge you. people who are native speakers are going to appreciate that you're trying to speak their language. Just put away your pride or your fears and just try.
>> I also would like to give you some feedback.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: >> I want to add something. If you do not understand certain words, please don't be afraid to ask because that shows that you're listening, >> right? You will learn something new and the other person will also feel like you are engaged in the conversation so they will not be offended.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: I'm walking to the office now. I was told that Access Y employees have flexible working hours, which is really great because I don't have to clock in at a certain time.

>> Hey Kelly. Hi. So, one thing we're going to work on is finding 30 to 35 influencers. You can go through some of the skin care and beauty focused influencers. title: have something done
examples: >> So, here we have our branded cafe. A lot of people come here to get coffee and to get our amazing dessert and everything.
>> Can we have a look at what you worked on today?
>> Okay. Let's have a look at what you did.
